description: One of the main commercial streets in Enshi City, featuring numerous local specialty restaurants and shops, making it an excellent destination to savor Enshi cuisine.
The largest Tujia cultural commercial pedestrian street in the country, a 4A-level scenic spot, offering free access to experience Tujia customs.
A deep fissure within Enshi Grand Canyon, stretching 20 kilometers with an average depth of 100 meters, featuring spectacular waterfalls.
A large ancient architectural complex built during the late Ming and early Qing dynasties, including the Li family manor and ancestral hall, showcasing ancient Tujia architectural artistry.
A landmark ancient pagoda in Enshi City, located atop Wufeng Mountain, offering a panoramic view of Enshi City.
A large-scale Tujia song and dance performance inside Tenglong Cave, incorporating laser shows and traditional folk culture.
A comprehensive museum showcasing the history, geological features, and ethnic customs of the Enshi region.
One of the largest karst caves in the world, a national 5A-level scenic spot, featuring vast interior spaces and an underground river.
A large-scale folk scenic area showcasing Tujia culture, a 4A-level scenic spot, offering an immersive experience of Tujia architecture and traditions.
